**[CONFIDENTIAL]{.underline}**

 

**[Summary of Lender Appraisal]{.underline}**

**[Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc.]{.underline}**

**[Total Enterprise Valuation]{.underline}**

 

 

**Prepared for:** Cartwright National Bank, N.A., as Administrative Agent

**Valuation Date:** May 15, 2025

**Prepared by:** Valuation Advisory Group of Cartwright National Bank, N.A.

 

1600 Arch Street, 30th Floor Philadelphia, PA 19103

 

 

This memorandum contains confidential and proprietary information of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. and is subject to the restrictions on distribution and use set forth in Section VI herein. This document may not be reproduced, in whole or in part, or distributed to any person or entity other than the intended recipients without the prior written consent of Cartwright National Bank, N.A.

**[I. Purpose and Scope]{.underline}**

This memorandum summarizes the independent appraisal of the Total Enterprise Value (\"TEV\") of Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc. (\"Meridian\" or the \"Company\"), a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 4200 Tryon Industrial Parkway, Charlotte, NC 28217 (EIN: 84-3927561). The appraisal was performed by the Valuation Advisory Group of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. (\"Cartwright\" or the \"Bank\"), applying methodologies and procedures consistent with the Bank\'s internal valuation policies and generally accepted valuation principles.

The appraisal was commissioned by Cartwright in its capacity as Administrative Agent for the proposed \$475,000,000 First Lien Credit Facility (the \"First Lien Facility\"), in connection with the Company\'s refinancing of its existing senior secured credit facility, which matures on August 15, 2025. The refinancing transaction contemplates a new capital structure consisting of the First Lien Facility and a \$150,000,000 Second Lien Term Loan B (the \"Second Lien Facility\" and, together with the First Lien Facility, the \"Credit Facilities\"). The Second Lien Facility is being arranged by Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP (\"Ridgeline\"), acting as Second Lien Agent.

The valuation is intended for use by the first lien lending syndicate organized by Cartwright and, as applicable, the second lien lending group led by Ridgeline, for the following purposes: (a) underwriting and credit approval of the proposed Credit Facilities; (b) assessment of loan-to-value ratios and credit metrics in connection with internal risk rating and regulatory compliance; and (c) intercreditor negotiations between the first lien and second lien lending groups, including the establishment of appropriate equity cushion thresholds under the proposed Intercreditor Agreement.

The valuation date for purposes of this appraisal is May 15, 2025 (the \"Valuation Date\"). All financial data, market observations, and comparable company metrics utilized in this analysis are as of or for the periods ending on or about the Valuation Date, unless otherwise noted. The Company\'s financial results are presented on a last-twelve-months (\"LTM\") basis through the fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2025.

Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc. is a diversified industrial manufacturer and services provider operating across multiple end markets, including general industrial, infrastructure, and energy-related sectors. The Company operates through a network of manufacturing and service facilities located primarily in the southeastern and midwestern United States. As of the Valuation Date, Meridian employs approximately 2,400 full-time equivalent employees and generates revenue through a combination of engineered products, aftermarket parts and services, and long-term maintenance contracts.

**[II. Conclusion of Value]{.underline}**

Based upon the analyses described herein, the Valuation Advisory Group of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. has concluded that the appraised Total Enterprise Value of Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc. is approximately **\$640,000,000** (six hundred forty million dollars) as of the Valuation Date of May 15, 2025.

This figure represents the midpoint of a valuation range of **\$620,000,000 to \$660,000,000**. The concluded TEV is derived from a blended analysis that weights the two primary valuation methodologies as follows:

> • **Discounted Cash Flow (\"DCF\") Analysis:** weighted at **50%**, yielding an indicated TEV range of \$615,000,000 to \$655,000,000; and
>
> • **Comparable Company Trading Multiples Analysis:** weighted at **50%**, yielding an indicated TEV range (after adjustments) of \$620,000,000 to \$660,000,000.

The equal weighting of these two methodologies reflects the Valuation Advisory Group\'s assessment that both approaches provide meaningful and reliable indications of value for a company of Meridian\'s profile. The DCF analysis captures the Company\'s projected cash flow generation capacity and forward-looking operating trajectory, while the comparable company analysis anchors the valuation in observable market data reflecting current investor expectations for similarly situated industrial businesses.

The concluded TEV of \$640,000,000 implies the following credit metrics based on the proposed capital structure of the Credit Facilities:

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Metric**                         **Calculation**                                            **Result**
  ---------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------
  Total Secured Debt                 \$475,000,000 (First Lien) + \$150,000,000 (Second Lien)   \$625,000,000

  Total Loan-to-Value (\"LTV\")      \$625,000,000 ÷ \$640,000,000                              **97.66%**

  First Lien LTV                     \$475,000,000 ÷ \$640,000,000                              **74.22%**

  Second Lien Equity Cushion         (\$640,000,000 − \$475,000,000) ÷ \$640,000,000            **25.78%**

  First Lien Leverage (LTM EBITDA)   \$475,000,000 ÷ \$89,400,000                               **5.31x**

  Total Leverage (LTM EBITDA)        \$625,000,000 ÷ \$89,400,000                               **6.99x**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Total LTV of approximately 97.66% indicates that the aggregate amount of first lien and second lien secured debt represents substantially all of the concluded TEV, leaving limited residual equity value beneath the second lien tranche. The First Lien LTV of approximately 74.22% provides meaningful asset coverage for the first lien lending syndicate, with approximately \$165,000,000 of implied enterprise value in excess of the First Lien Facility. The Second Lien equity cushion of approximately 25.78% represents the percentage of TEV available to absorb value deterioration before the first lien lenders\' recovery is impaired, and is a key metric for the intercreditor negotiations between Cartwright and Ridgeline.

The leverage multiples noted above---5.31x for the First Lien Facility and 6.99x on a total basis---are consistent with leveraged credit transactions in the industrial sector, though they reflect a capital structure that is positioned at the upper end of the range for companies with Meridian\'s operating profile and margin characteristics. These metrics should be evaluated in the context of the Company\'s cash flow generation capacity, capital expenditure requirements, and debt service coverage ratios as detailed in the credit memorandum prepared separately by Cartwright\'s Leveraged Finance Group.

**[III. Discounted Cash Flow Analysis]{.underline}**

The Valuation Advisory Group performed a discounted cash flow analysis based on a five-year projection period spanning the fiscal years ending December 31, 2025 through December 31, 2029 (the \"Projection Period\"). The projections utilized in the DCF analysis are based on Meridian management\'s business plan and financial forecasts, as furnished to Cartwright in connection with the refinancing, and as adjusted by the Valuation Advisory Group to reflect the Bank\'s independent assessment of achievability, end-market conditions, and macroeconomic assumptions. The adjustments made by the Valuation Advisory Group were informed by consultations with Cartwright\'s industry specialists in the industrial and manufacturing sectors, as well as publicly available market data and third-party research.

The key assumptions underlying the DCF analysis are summarized below:

**Revenue Growth.** Projected revenue is assumed to grow at a compound annual growth rate (\"CAGR\") of approximately **4.0% to 5.5%** over the Projection Period. This growth assumption reflects the Company\'s current backlog visibility, which as of the Valuation Date stood at approximately \$285,000,000 representing roughly 5.5 months of trailing revenue, as well as management\'s identified pipeline of new customer engagements and contract renewals. The growth rate also takes into account end-market conditions in the industrial sector, including modest expansion in non-residential construction activity, sustained infrastructure spending driven by federal appropriations, and stable demand trends in the energy-related maintenance and services segments. The Valuation Advisory Group applied a modestly conservative adjustment to the upper end of management\'s revenue forecast to account for execution risk associated with certain new product introductions and geographic expansion initiatives planned for fiscal years 2027 and 2028.

**EBITDA Margins.** Projected EBITDA margins are assumed to stabilize in the range of **14.5% to 16.0%** over the Projection Period, consistent with the Company\'s recent operating performance. The Company\'s LTM EBITDA margin as of the Valuation Date was approximately 14.8%, calculated on LTM EBITDA of \$89,400,000 and implied LTM revenue of approximately \$604,100,000. Management\'s projections assume modest margin improvement over the Projection Period driven by operating leverage on incremental revenue, procurement savings from recently renegotiated supply agreements, and the expected contribution of higher-margin aftermarket and services revenue as a proportion of the total revenue mix. The Valuation Advisory Group accepted management\'s margin assumptions at the lower end of the projected range and applied a haircut to the upper-end scenario reflecting potential headwinds from labor cost inflation and raw material price volatility, both of which have exhibited heightened uncertainty in the current macroeconomic environment.

**Discount Rate / WACC.** The projected free cash flows were discounted to present value using a weighted average cost of capital (\"WACC\") in the range of **10.5% to 11.5%**. The WACC was derived using a build-up methodology incorporating the following components:

> • Risk-free rate based on the 20-year U.S. Treasury yield of approximately 4.85% as of the analysis date;
>
> • Equity risk premium of 5.50%, based on the Duff & Phelps (Kroll) recommended equity risk premium as of the first quarter of 2025;
>
> • Size premium of 1.75%, reflecting Meridian\'s positioning as a mid-market industrial company with a TEV below \$1 billion;
>
> • Company-specific risk premium of 1.00% to 1.50%, reflecting execution risk associated with the Company\'s growth initiatives, customer concentration considerations, and the leveraged nature of the proposed capital structure;
>
> • Pre-tax cost of debt of approximately 8.25% to 8.75%, reflecting the anticipated pricing on the proposed Credit Facilities (SOFR + 300 to 350 basis points on the First Lien Facility), with the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (\"SOFR\") at 5.33% as of the analysis date; and
>
> • Target capital structure weighting of 60% debt / 40% equity, based on the proposed capital structure and observed capital structures of comparable companies in the industrial sector.

The resulting WACC range of 10.5% to 11.5% is consistent with discount rates applied in recent leveraged lending appraisals for similarly situated industrial companies within Cartwright\'s portfolio and in the broader leveraged finance market.

**Terminal Value.** The terminal value, representing the estimated enterprise value of Meridian at the end of the Projection Period, was calculated using a **perpetuity growth method** with a long-term growth rate of **2.5%**. This long-term growth rate is intended to approximate the expected long-run nominal growth rate of the U.S. economy and the Company\'s addressable end markets, and is within the range generally accepted in valuation practice for mature industrial businesses.

As a reasonableness check, the terminal value derived from the perpetuity growth method was cross-referenced against a terminal value implied by an **EV/EBITDA exit multiple of 7.0x to 7.5x** applied to the projected terminal year (2029) EBITDA. The two approaches yielded terminal value indications within an acceptable convergence range, providing the Valuation Advisory Group with confidence in the reasonableness of the terminal value assumptions. The exit multiple range of 7.0x to 7.5x is consistent with the current trading multiples observed for the comparable company peer group, as discussed in Section IV below, and reflects a conservative assumption that the Company\'s exit valuation will not benefit from meaningful multiple expansion relative to current market levels.

**DCF-Indicated TEV Range.** The discounted cash flow analysis, incorporating the assumptions described above and reflecting the present value of projected unlevered free cash flows during the Projection Period plus the present value of the terminal value, yields an indicated Total Enterprise Value range of **\$615,000,000 to \$655,000,000**. The midpoint of this range is approximately \$635,000,000.

The sensitivity of the DCF valuation to key assumptions was tested across a matrix of discount rates and terminal growth rates. Under the base case assumptions (WACC of 11.0% and terminal growth rate of 2.5%), the DCF-indicated TEV is approximately \$635,000,000. A 50 basis point increase in the discount rate, holding all other assumptions constant, reduces the indicated TEV by approximately \$25,000,000 to \$30,000,000, while a 50 basis point decrease in the terminal growth rate reduces the indicated TEV by approximately \$15,000,000 to \$20,000,000. These sensitivities underscore the importance of the discount rate and terminal value assumptions to the concluded valuation and should be considered by the lending syndicate in evaluating downside scenarios.

**[IV. Comparable Company Analysis]{.underline}**

The Valuation Advisory Group selected a peer group of publicly traded industrial companies with operating characteristics, end-market exposure, revenue scale, and margin profiles broadly comparable to those of Meridian. The selected comparable companies operate in the diversified industrials, specialty manufacturing, and industrial services subsectors, and were chosen based on the following criteria: (a) revenue in the range of approximately \$300,000,000 to \$1,500,000,000; (b) EBITDA margins in the range of approximately 12.0% to 20.0%; (c) meaningful exposure to infrastructure, general industrial, and/or energy-related end markets; and (d) publicly available financial data as of or near the Valuation Date. Companies with significant non-industrial business segments, those undergoing material restructurings, and those subject to pending change-of-control transactions were excluded from the peer group to avoid distortive effects on observed trading multiples.

The key observations from the comparable company analysis are as follows:

**Median EV/LTM EBITDA Multiple.** The selected comparable companies exhibited a **median EV/LTM EBITDA multiple of 7.0x to 7.5x** as of the analysis date. The range reflects variability within the peer group based on differences in growth profiles, margin quality, geographic diversification, and business mix. The interquartile range of observed multiples was approximately 6.5x to 8.2x, indicating a moderately dispersed dataset. The Valuation Advisory Group focused on the median range, rather than the mean, to minimize the impact of outlier observations at either end of the distribution.

**Application to Meridian\'s LTM EBITDA.** The selected multiple range of 7.0x to 7.5x was applied to Meridian\'s LTM EBITDA of **\$89,400,000**, yielding an **unadjusted indicated TEV range of approximately \$625,800,000 to \$670,500,000**. This range represents the implied enterprise value of Meridian if it were valued at the median trading multiples of the selected peer group, before consideration of any company-specific adjustments.

**Adjustments.** The Valuation Advisory Group applied the following adjustments to the unadjusted indicated TEV range to arrive at a company-specific valuation indication:

> • *Size Discount:* A modest downward adjustment was applied to reflect the fact that Meridian, as a privately held middle-market company, is smaller in scale and has a less diversified revenue base than many of the publicly traded comparables in the peer group. Smaller companies generally trade at lower multiples due to higher perceived risk, more limited access to capital markets, and greater vulnerability to customer and supplier concentration.
>
> • *Liquidity Considerations:* An additional adjustment was made to account for the lack of public market liquidity for Meridian\'s equity interests. While the lender appraisal is conducted on an enterprise value basis rather than an equity value basis, the absence of a liquid public market for the Company\'s securities is a relevant consideration in assessing appropriate valuation multiples, as public company multiples inherently reflect the liquidity premium associated with publicly traded securities.
>
> • *Company-Specific Risk Factors:* The Valuation Advisory Group considered certain company-specific risk factors that may warrant a valuation adjustment relative to the peer group median, including the Company\'s customer concentration profile (with the top five customers representing approximately 32% of LTM revenue), geographic concentration in the southeastern United States, and the capital-intensive nature of certain of the Company\'s manufacturing operations, which may constrain free cash flow conversion relative to asset-lighter peer companies.

After application of these adjustments, the **comparable company-indicated TEV range (adjusted) is \$620,000,000 to \$660,000,000**. The midpoint of this adjusted range is \$640,000,000, which is consistent with the midpoint indicated by the DCF analysis and provides corroborative support for the concluded TEV.

The Valuation Advisory Group notes that the comparable company analysis is inherently limited by the availability of truly comparable publicly traded companies and by the fact that public market valuations can be influenced by factors not directly relevant to a private company lender appraisal, including public market sentiment, index inclusion effects, and trading volume dynamics. Accordingly, the comparable company analysis is used in conjunction with, rather than as a substitute for, the DCF analysis in arriving at the concluded TEV.

**[V. Key Assumptions and Limiting Conditions]{.underline}**

The appraisal summarized herein is subject to the following key assumptions, qualifications, and limiting conditions, which should be carefully reviewed by all recipients of this memorandum:

**Reliance on Company-Provided Information.** The appraisal relies upon financial statements, management projections, operational data, asset schedules, backlog reports, and other information furnished by Meridian management and reviewed by Cartwright\'s Valuation Advisory Group and Leveraged Finance Group. These materials have not been independently audited or verified for purposes of this analysis. The Valuation Advisory Group has assumed, without independent verification, the accuracy and completeness of all information provided by the Company. While the Valuation Advisory Group has performed reasonableness checks on key financial data points and has made certain adjustments to management\'s projections as described herein, the reliability of the concluded valuation is dependent upon the accuracy and completeness of the underlying information furnished by the Company.

**LTM EBITDA.** The valuation relies upon a last-twelve-months EBITDA figure of **\$89,400,000** as reported by the Company for the twelve-month period ended March 31, 2025. This figure has been reviewed by Cartwright\'s Leveraged Finance Group for consistency with the Company\'s audited financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 and the unaudited financial statements for the quarter ended March 31, 2025. EBITDA as used in this analysis represents earnings before interest expense, income taxes, depreciation, and amortization, and may include certain adjustments for non-recurring items as agreed between the Company and Cartwright; however, the definition of EBITDA applied in this appraisal may differ from the definition of \"Consolidated EBITDA\" or \"Adjusted EBITDA\" as defined in the credit agreement for the proposed Credit Facilities.

**Going-Concern Basis.** The valuation assumes a **going-concern basis** and reflects the estimated value of Meridian as a continuing operating enterprise. The concluded TEV does not reflect liquidation value, forced-sale value, or orderly wind-down value, and should not be interpreted as an indication of the proceeds that would be realized in a distressed sale, bankruptcy proceeding, or other forced disposition of the Company\'s assets. The going-concern assumption presumes that the Company will continue to operate in the ordinary course, will have access to adequate working capital and liquidity, and will not be subject to any material adverse change in its business, financial condition, or regulatory environment that would fundamentally impair its ability to operate.

**No Physical Inspection.** No physical inspection of the Company\'s real property, manufacturing facilities, equipment, or other tangible assets was conducted in connection with this appraisal. The analysis relies on asset schedules, depreciation records, and capital expenditure histories provided by the Company, supplemented by publicly available information regarding comparable industrial properties and equipment. The Valuation Advisory Group makes no representation regarding the physical condition, environmental compliance, or replacement cost of the Company\'s assets.

**Not a Fairness Opinion.** This summary memorandum does not constitute a fairness opinion and should not be construed as providing any opinion or recommendation as to the fairness, from a financial point of view, of the terms of the proposed Credit Facilities or any other transaction involving the Company. The purpose of this appraisal is solely to provide an independent estimate of TEV for use in connection with credit underwriting, risk assessment, and intercreditor analysis, and does not address the adequacy of consideration, the merits of the proposed transaction, or the financial interests of any particular party.

**Subject to Change.** The valuation presented herein is based on conditions existing as of the Valuation Date of May 15, 2025, and is subject to change based on, among other things, subsequent movements in market conditions, changes in the interest rate environment (including changes in the SOFR reference rate), fluctuations in public equity market valuations and comparable company trading multiples, updated financial performance of the Company, changes in the Company\'s competitive position or end-market conditions, and any material events or developments occurring after the Valuation Date. The Valuation Advisory Group undertakes no obligation to update or revise this appraisal for events or circumstances arising after the Valuation Date, unless separately engaged to do so.

**Debt-Free, Cash-Free Basis.** The concluded TEV is presented on a **debt-free, cash-free basis**, representing the value of the Company\'s total invested capital without regard to the Company\'s existing or proposed capital structure, cash balances, or net working capital adjustments. The conversion of TEV to equity value, if applicable, would require adjustments for outstanding indebtedness, cash and cash equivalents, and other balance sheet items, which are beyond the scope of this appraisal.

**Governing Law.** The engagement terms under which this appraisal was performed, including confidentiality obligations, limitation of liability provisions, and dispute resolution mechanisms, are governed by the laws of the State of **New York**, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.

**[VI. Distribution and Use]{.underline}**

This summary memorandum is provided solely for the use of Cartwright National Bank, N.A., in its capacity as Administrative Agent under the proposed First Lien Facility, and the syndicate of first lien lenders participating in the First Lien Facility. In addition, this memorandum is made available to Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP, in its capacity as Second Lien Agent under the proposed Second Lien Facility, and the participating second lien lenders, in each case solely in connection with the proposed Credit Facilities and the intercreditor arrangements being negotiated between the first lien and second lien lending groups for Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc.

This memorandum may not be used for any purpose other than the evaluation, underwriting, credit approval, and administration of the proposed Credit Facilities and the related intercreditor negotiations. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, this memorandum may not be used in connection with any public offering, securities filing, regulatory submission, litigation proceeding, or other purpose without the prior written consent of Cartwright National Bank, N.A.

Redistribution, reproduction, or dissemination of this memorandum, in whole or in part, to any person or entity other than the intended recipients described above, is strictly prohibited without the prior written consent of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. Each recipient of this memorandum agrees, by accepting delivery hereof, to be bound by the foregoing restrictions on distribution and use.

The Valuation Advisory Group of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. expressly disclaims any liability to any person or entity other than the intended recipients of this memorandum for any losses, damages, or claims arising out of or in connection with the use of, or reliance upon, the information, analyses, or conclusions contained herein.

**[Certification]{.underline}**

The undersigned, in his capacity as a senior officer of the Valuation Advisory Group of Cartwright National Bank, N.A., hereby certifies that (a) the analyses, opinions, and conclusions set forth in this summary memorandum were prepared in accordance with the internal valuation policies and procedures of Cartwright National Bank, N.A.; (b) the Valuation Advisory Group has no present or contemplated financial interest in Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc. or any of its affiliates; (c) the compensation received by the Valuation Advisory Group for the preparation of this appraisal is not contingent upon the conclusions of value stated herein or the consummation of the proposed Credit Facilities; and (d) the analyses, opinions, and conclusions stated herein represent the independent professional judgment of the Valuation Advisory Group as of the Valuation Date of May 15, 2025.
