**[ARTICLE 1 --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 --- Definitions**

As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the following meanings:

\"**Bankruptcy Code**\" means Title 11 of the United States Code (11 U.S.C. §§ 101 *et seq.*), as amended, modified, succeeded, or replaced from time to time.

\"**Borrower**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in the preamble hereto.

\"**Business Day**\" means any day other than a Saturday, a Sunday, or a day on which commercial banks in New York City are authorized or required by law, regulation, or executive order to close.

\"**Collateral**\" means all assets and property of the Grantors, whether real, personal, or mixed, and whether now owned or hereafter acquired, that are or may at any time be subject to Liens securing the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations, or both, pursuant to any First Lien Collateral Document or Second Lien Collateral Document, as applicable.

\"**Common Collateral**\" means, at any time, Collateral in which both the First Lien Secured Parties and the Second Lien Secured Parties hold a Lien at such time. For the avoidance of doubt, if the Lien of only the First Lien Secured Parties or only the Second Lien Secured Parties attaches to or is perfected in any asset, such asset shall not constitute Common Collateral.

\"**DIP Financing**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in Section 7.2 hereof.

\"**Discharge of First Lien Obligations**\" means the first date on which each of the following conditions shall have been satisfied: (a) all First Lien Obligations (other than unasserted contingent indemnification obligations and unasserted contingent expense reimbursement obligations that by their terms survive termination of the First Lien Documents and letters of credit that have been cash collateralized or backstopped to the satisfaction of the issuing lender thereof) have been indefeasibly paid in full in cash; (b) all commitments to extend credit under the First Lien Documents have been irrevocably terminated; and (c) all letters of credit issued under the First Lien Documents have been terminated, have expired, have been cash collateralized, or have been backstopped, in each case, on terms reasonably satisfactory to the applicable issuing lender thereof.

\"**Discharge of Second Lien Obligations**\" means the first date on which each of the following conditions shall have been satisfied: (a) all Second Lien Obligations (other than unasserted contingent indemnification obligations and unasserted contingent expense reimbursement obligations that by their terms survive termination of the Second Lien Documents) have been indefeasibly paid in full in cash; and (b) all commitments to extend credit under the Second Lien Documents have been irrevocably terminated.

\"**Enforcement Action**\" means the exercise of any rights and remedies with respect to any Common Collateral, whether under any First Lien Document, any Second Lien Document, applicable law, or otherwise, including (a) the commencement or prosecution of any foreclosure action or any other action or proceeding for the realization upon, collection against, or liquidation of any Common Collateral, (b) the exercise of any right of set-off, recoupment, or netting against or with respect to any Common Collateral, (c) the commencement of any Insolvency Proceeding against the Borrower or any Grantor, (d) the taking of any action to enforce, collect upon, or realize against any Lien on any Common Collateral, (e) the notification of account debtors or obligors of any assignment of any Common Collateral, and (f) the exercise of any right under Article 9 of the UCC or under any equivalent provision of any other applicable law with respect to any Common Collateral; *provided* that \"Enforcement Action\" shall not include (i) the filing of a proof of claim or statement of interest in any Insolvency Proceeding, (ii) the filing of any responsive or defensive pleading in any Insolvency Proceeding or other judicial or administrative proceeding, or (iii) the acceleration of the maturity of any obligations.

\"**First Lien Agent**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in the preamble hereto.

\"**First Lien Cap**\" means, at any time, the sum of (a) the aggregate outstanding principal amount of the First Lien Obligations at such time, plus (b) all accrued and unpaid interest (including default interest) thereon, plus (c) all fees, premiums, expenses, indemnification obligations, and all other amounts owing under the First Lien Documents at such time; *provided* that for the avoidance of doubt, the First Lien Cap shall include any increase in the aggregate principal amount of the First Lien Obligations resulting from protective advances or revolving credit extensions to the extent permitted under the First Lien Credit Agreement as in effect on the date hereof (or as amended in accordance with the terms of this Agreement).

\"**First Lien Collateral Documents**\" means, collectively, the security agreements, pledge agreements, mortgages, deeds of trust, intellectual property security agreements, deposit account control agreements, securities account control agreements, and all other agreements, instruments, and documents now or hereafter granting or purporting to grant Liens on any Collateral to secure any or all of the First Lien Obligations.

\"**First Lien Credit Agreement**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in the Recitals hereto.

\"**First Lien Documents**\" means, collectively, the First Lien Credit Agreement, the First Lien Collateral Documents, and all other agreements, instruments, certificates, notes, guarantees, and other documents executed and delivered in connection with the First Lien Credit Agreement or the First Lien Obligations, in each case as the same may be amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time in accordance with their terms and the terms of this Agreement.

\"**First Lien Obligations**\" means all obligations of the Borrower and the Grantors under or in connection with the First Lien Documents, howsoever created, arising, or evidenced, whether direct or indirect, absolute or contingent, now or hereafter existing, or due or to become due, including (a) all principal, (b) interest (including interest accruing on or after the filing of any petition in any Insolvency Proceeding, whether or not such interest is allowed or allowable as a claim in such Insolvency Proceeding), (c) premiums, (d) fees, (e) costs and expenses (including attorneys\' fees and disbursements), (f) indemnification obligations, (g) breakage costs, (h) reimbursement obligations with respect to letters of credit, (i) hedging obligations to the extent secured under the First Lien Collateral Documents, and (j) all other amounts payable under or in connection with the First Lien Documents.

\"**First Lien Secured Parties**\" means the First Lien Agent and each lender, issuing bank, hedge counterparty, cash management bank, and other holder of First Lien Obligations from time to time.

\"**Grantors**\" means, collectively, the Borrower and each subsidiary, affiliate, or other Person that has granted, or may hereafter grant, a Lien on any of its assets to secure the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations, or both.

\"**Insolvency Proceeding**\" means (a) any case, action, or proceeding before any court or other governmental authority relating to bankruptcy, reorganization, insolvency, liquidation, receivership, dissolution, winding-up, or relief of debtors under the Bankruptcy Code or any other federal, state, or foreign bankruptcy, insolvency, receivership, or similar law now or hereafter in effect, (b) any proceeding for the appointment of a receiver, trustee, liquidator, custodian, sequestrator, conservator, or similar official for any Person or for any substantial part of such Person\'s assets, or (c) any general assignment for the benefit of creditors.

\"**Lien**\" means any mortgage, deed of trust, pledge, hypothecation, assignment (whether for security or otherwise), security interest, lien (whether statutory or otherwise), charge, claim, or encumbrance of any kind, whether voluntary or involuntary, howsoever arising, including any conditional sale or other title retention agreement, any lease in the nature thereof, and any agreement to give or grant any of the foregoing.

\"**Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in Section 6.1 hereof.

\"**Person**\" means any individual, corporation, general or limited partnership, limited liability company, joint venture, estate, trust, association, organization, governmental authority, or other entity of any kind or nature.

\"**Purchase Notice**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in Section 5.1 hereof.

\"**Purchase Right**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in Section 5.1 hereof.

\"**Second Lien Agent**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in the preamble hereto.

\"**Second Lien Collateral Documents**\" means, collectively, the security agreements, pledge agreements, mortgages, deeds of trust, intellectual property security agreements, deposit account control agreements, securities account control agreements, and all other agreements, instruments, and documents now or hereafter granting or purporting to grant Liens on any Collateral to secure any or all of the Second Lien Obligations.

\"**Second Lien Credit Agreement**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in the Recitals hereto.

\"**Second Lien Documents**\" means, collectively, the Second Lien Credit Agreement, the Second Lien Collateral Documents, and all other agreements, instruments, certificates, notes, guarantees, and other documents executed and delivered in connection with the Second Lien Credit Agreement or the Second Lien Obligations, in each case as the same may be amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time in accordance with their terms and the terms of this Agreement.

\"**Second Lien Obligations**\" means all obligations of the Borrower and the Grantors under or in connection with the Second Lien Documents, howsoever created, arising, or evidenced, whether direct or indirect, absolute or contingent, now or hereafter existing, or due or to become due, including (a) all principal, (b) interest (including interest accruing on or after the filing of any petition in any Insolvency Proceeding, whether or not such interest is allowed or allowable as a claim in such Insolvency Proceeding), (c) premiums, (d) fees, (e) costs and expenses (including attorneys\' fees and disbursements), (f) indemnification obligations, (g) breakage costs, and (h) all other amounts payable under or in connection with the Second Lien Documents.

\"**Second Lien Secured Parties**\" means the Second Lien Agent and each lender and other holder of Second Lien Obligations from time to time.

\"**Standstill Period**\" has the meaning assigned to such term in Section 3.1 hereof.

\"**UCC**\" means the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect from time to time in the State of New York; *provided* that if, by reason of mandatory provisions of applicable law, the validity, perfection, priority, or enforcement of any Lien on any Common Collateral is governed by the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect in a jurisdiction other than the State of New York, \"UCC\" means the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect from time to time in such other jurisdiction for purposes of the provisions hereof relating to such validity, perfection, priority, or enforcement.

**Section 1.2 --- Terms Generally**

\(a\) Terms used in this Agreement but not otherwise defined herein shall have the meanings given to such terms in the First Lien Credit Agreement as in effect on the date hereof or, if not defined therein, in the UCC.

\(b\) The definitions of terms herein shall apply equally to the singular and plural forms of the terms defined. Whenever the context may require, any pronoun shall include the corresponding masculine, feminine, and neuter forms.

\(c\) The words \"include,\" \"includes,\" and \"including\" shall be deemed to be followed by the phrase \"without limitation.\" The word \"will\" shall be construed to have the same meaning and effect as the word \"shall.\"

\(d\) Unless the context requires otherwise, (i) any definition of or reference to any agreement, instrument, or other document herein shall be construed as referring to such agreement, instrument, or other document as from time to time amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified (subject to any restrictions on such amendments, restatements, supplements, or modifications set forth herein or in any other applicable agreement), (ii) any reference herein to any Person shall be construed to include such Person\'s successors and permitted assigns, (iii) the words \"herein,\" \"hereof,\" and \"hereunder,\" and words of similar import, shall be construed to refer to this Agreement in its entirety and not to any particular provision hereof, and (iv) all references herein to Sections and Articles shall be construed to refer to Sections and Articles of this Agreement unless otherwise specified.

\(e\) Any reference herein to any law, statute, rule, or regulation shall be construed as referring to such law, statute, rule, or regulation as amended, modified, codified, reenacted, supplemented, or superseded in whole or in part and in effect from time to time, and also to all rules and regulations promulgated thereunder.

\(f\) Any reference herein to \"the date hereof\" or \"the date of this Agreement\" shall refer to the date set forth in the preamble hereto.

**[ARTICLE 2 --- LIEN PRIORITIES AND SUBORDINATION]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- Subordination of Second Liens**

\(a\) Notwithstanding the date, time, method, manner, or order of grant, attachment, or perfection of any Liens granted on the Common Collateral to secure the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations, and notwithstanding any provision of the UCC, any other applicable law, any First Lien Document, or any Second Lien Document, or any other circumstance whatsoever, all Liens on Common Collateral securing the Second Lien Obligations now or hereafter held by or on behalf of the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Secured Party are and shall be in all respects subordinate and junior to all Liens on Common Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations now or hereafter held by or on behalf of the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Secured Party.

\(b\) The subordination of the Liens on Common Collateral securing the Second Lien Obligations to the Liens on Common Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations set forth in this Section 2.1 shall apply and be effective irrespective of, and each of the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties hereby waives any right to assert otherwise based upon:

> \(i\) the date, time, or order of filing or recording of any financing statement, mortgage, deed of trust, or other document filed or recorded to perfect any Lien on any Common Collateral;
>
> \(ii\) any deficiency or defect in the creation, attachment, or perfection of any Lien granted on any Common Collateral to secure the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations;
>
> \(iii\) any provision of the UCC, any federal, state, or foreign law, or any First Lien Document or Second Lien Document;
>
> \(iv\) the fact that any Lien on Common Collateral securing any First Lien Obligation is or may be unperfected, subject to avoidance, subordinated, recharacterized, or otherwise impaired or invalidated;
>
> \(v\) the fact that any of the First Lien Obligations may be subordinated, recharacterized, or otherwise impaired or invalidated; or
>
> \(vi\) any other circumstance of any kind or nature whatsoever.

\(c\) The Liens on Common Collateral securing the Second Lien Obligations shall remain subordinate and junior to the Liens on Common Collateral securing the First Lien Obligations, in each case with the priority set forth in this Section 2.1, notwithstanding any amendment, modification, supplement, extension, renewal, restatement, replacement, or refinancing of either the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations (subject to the provisions of Article 6 regarding Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness), or any action or inaction that any party hereto or any First Lien Secured Party or Second Lien Secured Party may take or fail to take.

**Section 2.2 --- Prohibition on Contesting Liens**

\(a\) Each of the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties agrees that it shall not, and hereby waives any right to, directly or indirectly, contest, challenge, question, or raise any objection to (i) the validity, extent, perfection, priority, or enforceability of any Lien on any Common Collateral securing any First Lien Obligation, or (ii) the validity, enforceability, or allowability of any First Lien Obligation, or (iii) the right of the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Secured Party to exercise any right or remedy with respect to any Common Collateral under the First Lien Documents, applicable law, or this Agreement.

\(b\) Each of the First Lien Agent and the First Lien Secured Parties agrees that it shall not, and hereby waives any right to, directly or indirectly, contest, challenge, question, or raise any objection to the validity, extent, perfection, or enforceability of any Lien on any Common Collateral securing any Second Lien Obligation (but, for the avoidance of doubt, shall not be restricted from asserting and enforcing the subordination and other provisions of this Agreement).

**Section 2.3 --- No New Liens**

\(a\) If any Grantor shall at any time create or acquire any Lien on any asset or property to secure the First Lien Obligations, such Grantor shall concurrently therewith (or, if applicable, as promptly as practicable thereafter) grant a Lien on such asset or property to secure the Second Lien Obligations (subject to the terms and conditions of the Second Lien Documents and the subordination and other provisions of this Agreement).

\(b\) If any Grantor shall at any time create or acquire any Lien on any asset or property to secure the Second Lien Obligations, such Grantor shall concurrently therewith (or, if applicable, as promptly as practicable thereafter) grant a Lien on such asset or property to secure the First Lien Obligations (subject to the terms and conditions of the First Lien Documents and the subordination and other provisions of this Agreement).

\(c\) Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligations of the Grantors under this Section 2.3 shall be subject to any exclusions of assets or categories of assets expressly set forth in the applicable First Lien Documents or Second Lien Documents, as the case may be, from the collateral required to be pledged thereunder.

**Section 2.4 --- Perfection; Lien Priority Not Affected by Order of Filing**

The priority of the Liens as set forth in this Agreement shall not be altered or otherwise affected by (a) any amendment, modification, supplement, extension, renewal, restatement, replacement, or refinancing of either the First Lien Obligations or the Second Lien Obligations (subject to Article 6 hereof), (b) any action or inaction that any party hereto, any First Lien Secured Party, or any Second Lien Secured Party may take or fail to take with respect to the perfection or maintenance of perfection of any Lien on any Common Collateral, (c) the release of any Lien on any Common Collateral by any party hereto, or (d) any other circumstance whatsoever. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, no failure by the First Lien Agent to file or record any financing statement or other document, no failure to maintain the perfection of any Lien, and no deficiency in the perfection or continuation of any Lien shall affect the priorities set forth herein.

**Section 2.5 --- Similar Liens; Agreement to Be Bound**

\(a\) Each of the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent agrees that all future First Lien Collateral Documents and Second Lien Collateral Documents, respectively, shall include provisions acknowledging the subordination and other terms of this Agreement and providing that all Liens granted thereunder are subject to the terms of this Agreement.

\(b\) Each of the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent agrees that all future lenders, holders, or other secured parties that become parties to the First Lien Documents or Second Lien Documents, as applicable, after the date hereof shall, as a condition to becoming such a lender, holder, or secured party, execute and deliver a joinder agreement or other instrument in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the other Agent agreeing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the failure of any such future lender, holder, or secured party to execute and deliver such a joinder agreement shall not affect the binding nature of this Agreement upon such party, and each Agent agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause such party to execute and deliver such a joinder agreement.

**Section 2.6 --- Waiver of Marshalling and Similar Rights**

Each of the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties hereby waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any right it may have to require the marshalling of assets or any similar right or doctrine with respect to any Common Collateral. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, neither the Second Lien Agent nor any Second Lien Secured Party shall have the right to assert or enforce any right of marshalling, any right to require the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Secured Party to proceed against any particular item or category of Common Collateral before proceeding against any other item or category of Common Collateral, or any right to require the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Secured Party to pursue any particular remedy or course of action.

**[ARTICLE 3 --- STANDSTILL AND ENFORCEMENT LIMITATIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- Standstill Period**

\(a\) Until the Discharge of First Lien Obligations has occurred, the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall not take any Enforcement Action with respect to any Common Collateral, except as expressly provided in Section 3.2 and Section 3.4 hereof.

\(b\) Notwithstanding Section 3.1(a), the Second Lien Agent and Second Lien Secured Parties may take an Enforcement Action with respect to Common Collateral after the expiration of the Standstill Period. The \"**Standstill Period**\" means a period of one hundred eighty (180) days following the date on which the Second Lien Agent has delivered to the First Lien Agent a written notice (a \"**Standstill Trigger Notice**\") certifying (i) the occurrence and continuance of an event of default under the Second Lien Documents (specifying such event of default in reasonable detail), and (ii) the Second Lien Agent\'s intent to commence an Enforcement Action with respect to Common Collateral upon expiration of the Standstill Period.

\(c\) The Standstill Period shall be tolled, and shall not run, during the pendency of any Insolvency Proceeding with respect to the Borrower or any Grantor.

\(d\) For the avoidance of doubt, no Standstill Period shall commence or be in effect at any time when no event of default under the Second Lien Documents has occurred and is continuing. If the event of default specified in the applicable Standstill Trigger Notice is cured or waived at any time during the applicable Standstill Period, such Standstill Period shall immediately terminate and be of no further force or effect, and a new Standstill Period must be commenced by delivery of a new Standstill Trigger Notice in accordance with this Section 3.1 in connection with any subsequently occurring event of default.

**Section 3.2 --- Exceptions to Standstill; Permitted Actions by Second Lien Secured Parties**

Notwithstanding Section 3.1 or any other provision of this Agreement, at any time (whether or not the Standstill Period has commenced or expired, and whether or not the Discharge of First Lien Obligations has occurred), the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall be permitted to:

> \(a\) file a proof of claim or statement of interest in any Insolvency Proceeding, and amend, supplement, or otherwise modify any such proof of claim or statement of interest from time to time;
>
> \(b\) file any responsive or defensive pleading in opposition to any motion, claim, action, or proceeding that would be adverse to the interests of the Second Lien Secured Parties, including any pleading necessary to prevent the disallowance, avoidance, recharacterization, subordination (other than as contemplated by this Agreement), or impairment of the Second Lien Obligations or the Liens securing the same;
>
> \(c\) vote on any plan of reorganization in any Insolvency Proceeding, subject to the provisions of Section 7.4 hereof;
>
> \(d\) take any action to preserve or protect (but not to foreclose upon, realize on, or otherwise enforce) their Liens on Common Collateral, including the filing of financing statement continuation statements under the UCC, the recording of mortgage or deed of trust amendments or modifications, and the filing of any document necessary to maintain the registration or recording of any Lien;
>
> \(e\) accelerate the maturity of the Second Lien Obligations in accordance with the terms of the Second Lien Documents;
>
> \(f\) file an involuntary bankruptcy petition against the Borrower or any Grantor, *provided* that no such involuntary petition shall be filed prior to the expiration of the applicable Standstill Period;
>
> \(g\) exercise the Purchase Right set forth in Article 5 hereof; and
>
> \(h\) take any action required by applicable law, including any action required to comply with any court order or regulatory directive.

**Section 3.3 --- Exercise of Remedies by First Lien Agent**

Nothing in this Agreement shall restrict, impair, delay, or otherwise limit the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Secured Party from exercising, at any time and from time to time, any and all rights and remedies available to the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Secured Party under the First Lien Documents, applicable law, or otherwise with respect to any Common Collateral, including the right to commence and prosecute any Enforcement Action, to foreclose upon or otherwise realize on any Common Collateral, to credit bid in connection with any sale or disposition of any Common Collateral, and to exercise any and all other rights and remedies of a secured creditor under the UCC or any other applicable law. The First Lien Agent shall have the exclusive right to manage, perform, and enforce the terms of the First Lien Collateral Documents with respect to Common Collateral (subject to the rights of the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties set forth in Section 3.2 and Section 3.4 hereof and in Article 5 hereof), to determine the time and method of exercise of remedies, and to exercise or refrain from exercising any rights and remedies with respect thereto, in each case in the First Lien Agent\'s sole and absolute discretion.

**Section 3.4 --- Actions Upon Expiration of Standstill Period**

Upon the expiration of the Standstill Period (and *provided* that the Discharge of First Lien Obligations has not yet occurred), the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties may take any Enforcement Action with respect to Common Collateral, subject to the following conditions:

> \(a\) any and all Liens of the First Lien Secured Parties on such Common Collateral (and on any proceeds thereof) shall attach to any proceeds of any such Enforcement Action with the same priority as set forth in this Agreement, and the subordination and other provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect with respect to such proceeds;
>
> \(b\) all proceeds of any such Enforcement Action shall be applied in accordance with Article 4 hereof;
>
> \(c\) if the First Lien Agent has commenced an exercise of remedies with respect to all or any portion of the Common Collateral prior to the commencement of an Enforcement Action by the Second Lien Agent, the First Lien Agent shall retain the exclusive right to direct the conduct of any sale, disposition, or other realization on such Common Collateral, and the Second Lien Agent shall not take any action that would interfere with, delay, or impede the First Lien Agent\'s exercise of such remedies; and
>
> \(d\) the Second Lien Agent shall provide the First Lien Agent with not less than ten (10) Business Days\' prior written notice before commencing any Enforcement Action, which notice shall describe in reasonable detail the Enforcement Action to be taken.

**Section 3.5 --- Reinstatement of Standstill**

If, during the pendency of any Standstill Period, the event of default under the Second Lien Documents that gave rise to such Standstill Period is cured or waived in accordance with the terms of the Second Lien Documents, such Standstill Period shall immediately terminate. Any subsequent Enforcement Action by the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Secured Party shall require the delivery of a new Standstill Trigger Notice and the commencement and expiration of a new Standstill Period in accordance with Section 3.1 hereof. For the avoidance of doubt, there shall be no limit on the number of Standstill Periods that may be commenced or reinstituted under this Section 3.5, and a new Standstill Period may be commenced in connection with any event of default (including a previously cured and subsequently recurring event of default) as provided in Section 3.1.

**[ARTICLE 4 --- APPLICATION OF PROCEEDS]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Application of Enforcement Proceeds**

All proceeds received by any Person in connection with any Enforcement Action (including any sale, collection, foreclosure, disposition, or other realization upon any Common Collateral, whether by the First Lien Agent, the Second Lien Agent, or any other Person), whether received in cash, securities, or other property, shall be distributed and applied in the following order of priority (the \"**Enforcement Proceeds Waterfall**\"):

> **First**, to the payment of all costs and expenses incurred by the First Lien Agent in connection with such Enforcement Action, including the costs and expenses of any sale, collection, foreclosure, disposition, or other realization on Common Collateral, and all reasonable attorneys\' fees and disbursements, court costs, receiver\'s fees, and other expenses incurred by the First Lien Agent and the First Lien Secured Parties in connection therewith;
>
> **Second**, to the payment in full in cash of all First Lien Obligations (including, without limitation, all outstanding principal, all accrued and unpaid interest at the applicable contract rate or the default rate, as applicable, all fees, all premiums, all indemnities, and all other amounts due and owing under the First Lien Documents), in such order and manner of application as specified in the First Lien Credit Agreement or as otherwise directed by the First Lien Agent in its sole discretion, until the Discharge of First Lien Obligations has occurred;
>
> **Third**, to the payment of all costs and expenses incurred by the Second Lien Agent in connection with such Enforcement Action, including the costs and expenses of any sale, collection, foreclosure, disposition, or other realization on Common Collateral, and all reasonable attorneys\' fees and disbursements, court costs, and other expenses incurred by the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties in connection therewith;
>
> **Fourth**, to the payment in full in cash of all Second Lien Obligations (including, without limitation, all outstanding principal, all accrued and unpaid interest, all fees, all premiums, all indemnities, and all other amounts due and owing under the Second Lien Documents), in such order and manner of application as specified in the Second Lien Credit Agreement or as otherwise directed by the Second Lien Agent, until the Discharge of Second Lien Obligations has occurred; and
>
> **Fifth**, to the Borrower or to whomsoever may be lawfully entitled thereto, or as otherwise required by applicable law, including Section 9-615(d) of the UCC.

**Section 4.2 --- Application of Non-Enforcement Proceeds; Permitted Asset Dispositions and Casualty/Condemnation Proceeds**

\(a\) With respect to proceeds of (i) asset dispositions permitted under the First Lien Documents and the Second Lien Documents, in each case in the ordinary course or as otherwise expressly permitted, or (ii) casualty or condemnation events, in each case where such proceeds do not arise from or in connection with an Enforcement Action, such proceeds shall be applied first to the First Lien Obligations in accordance with the terms of the First Lien Documents, including any mandatory prepayment provisions set forth therein.

\(b\) To the extent the First Lien Documents require that such proceeds be applied to the prepayment of First Lien Obligations (whether on a mandatory or optional basis), such proceeds shall be so applied. After application of any such mandatory prepayment or other application required under the First Lien Documents, any remaining proceeds shall be applied in accordance with the terms of the Second Lien Documents.

\(c\) The Second Lien Agent shall release, and shall cause to be released, any Liens of the Second Lien Secured Parties on any Common Collateral that is the subject of any permitted asset disposition or casualty or condemnation event if and to the extent the First Lien Agent releases its Liens thereon in connection therewith (or has consented to such release), provided that the proceeds of such disposition, casualty, or condemnation are applied in accordance with this Section 4.2.

**Section 4.3 --- Turnover**

\(a\) If the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Secured Party shall at any time receive any proceeds of Common Collateral (whether by payment, distribution, setoff, or otherwise) other than as expressly permitted under Section 4.2 or Section 4.4 hereof, or after the Discharge of First Lien Obligations, then such Second Lien Secured Party shall hold such proceeds in trust for the benefit of the First Lien Secured Parties and shall promptly (and in any event within five (5) Business Days of receipt thereof) deliver such proceeds, in the form received (with any necessary endorsements or instruments of transfer), to the First Lien Agent for application in accordance with Section 4.1 hereof.

\(b\) Until so delivered to the First Lien Agent, any proceeds received by the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Secured Party and required to be turned over pursuant to this Section 4.3 shall be segregated from all other property of the receiving party and held in trust for the benefit of the First Lien Secured Parties.

**Section 4.4 --- Payments Permitted**

Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, until the Discharge of First Lien Obligations, the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall be permitted to receive, and the Borrower and the Grantors shall be permitted to make, (a) regularly scheduled payments of principal on the Second Lien Obligations, (b) regularly scheduled payments of interest on the Second Lien Obligations at the applicable non-default contract rate, and (c) regularly scheduled fees payable under the Second Lien Documents, in each case to the extent not prohibited by the terms of the Second Lien Documents or by applicable law (including the Bankruptcy Code) and to the extent such payments are made from funds other than proceeds of an Enforcement Action on Common Collateral; *provided* that no such payments shall be made at any time during the continuance of a payment default under the First Lien Documents with respect to scheduled payments of principal or interest on the First Lien Obligations.

**Section 4.5 --- Avoidance; Reinstatement**

To the extent any payment or distribution to the First Lien Secured Parties (or any of them) on account of the First Lien Obligations is subsequently invalidated, declared to be fraudulent or preferential, set aside, avoided, rescinded, or is otherwise required to be returned, restored, or repaid by any First Lien Secured Party under any Insolvency Proceeding or otherwise (whether pursuant to any settlement entered into by any First Lien Secured Party in its discretion or otherwise), the subordination, priority, and other provisions established by this Agreement shall be reinstated and deemed to be in full force and effect as though such payment or distribution had not been made. In such event, the Second Lien Obligations and the Liens securing the Second Lien Obligations shall be reinstated to the same priority and subordination as set forth in this Agreement, and the First Lien Obligations shall be deemed to be outstanding and unpaid to the extent of such avoidance, rescission, or requirement to return, as the case may be.

**[ARTICLE 5 --- PURCHASE RIGHT]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Purchase Right**

At any time following the occurrence of an event of default under the First Lien Documents, whether or not the First Lien Obligations have been accelerated or any Enforcement Action with respect to Common Collateral has been commenced by the First Lien Agent, the Second Lien Agent (on behalf of the Second Lien Secured Parties, or one or more Second Lien Secured Parties, or any designee of the foregoing) shall have the right, but not the obligation (the \"**Purchase Right**\"), to purchase all (but not less than all) of the First Lien Obligations from the First Lien Secured Parties at the Purchase Price (as defined in Section 5.2), by providing written notice of such election (the \"**Purchase Notice**\") to the First Lien Agent.

**Section 5.2 --- Purchase Price**

The purchase price for the First Lien Obligations (the \"**Purchase Price**\") shall be equal to the full amount of all First Lien Obligations outstanding on the closing date of the purchase, determined as of the close of business on the Business Day immediately preceding the closing date, including (a) all outstanding principal, (b) all accrued and unpaid interest (including default interest, if any), (c) all fees (including commitment fees, letter of credit fees, and all other fees), (d) all premiums (including any prepayment premiums or make-whole premiums), (e) all breakage costs, (f) all indemnification obligations that are then due and payable or that have been asserted in writing, (g) all costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and disbursements) incurred by the First Lien Agent and the First Lien Secured Parties in connection with the purchase and assignment, and (h) all other amounts then due and payable under the First Lien Documents. The Purchase Price shall be payable in full in cash on the closing date in immediately available funds.

**Section 5.3 --- Purchase Mechanics**

\(a\) The closing of the purchase of the First Lien Obligations pursuant to this Article 5 shall occur no later than fifteen (15) Business Days after the date of receipt of the Purchase Notice by the First Lien Agent, or such later date as may be necessary to obtain any required regulatory approval or consent (but in no event later than thirty (30) Business Days after such receipt), at such time and place as may be agreed upon by the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent.

\(b\) At the closing, the First Lien Agent and each First Lien Secured Party shall execute and deliver such assignment agreements, transfer documents, and other instruments as are reasonably requested by the purchasing party to evidence and effectuate the assignment and transfer of the First Lien Obligations, the First Lien Documents, and all Liens securing the First Lien Obligations to the purchasing party. All such assignments shall be substantially in the form of the assignment agreement set forth in the First Lien Credit Agreement or, if no such form is set forth therein, in a form reasonably acceptable to the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent.

\(c\) The purchase and assignment of the First Lien Obligations shall be made without representation, warranty, or recourse to the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Secured Party of any kind whatsoever, whether express or implied, except that each First Lien Secured Party shall represent and warrant that (i) it has the authority to assign its portion of the First Lien Obligations, (ii) it is the legal and beneficial owner of such First Lien Obligations free and clear of any Lien or encumbrance created by such First Lien Secured Party, and (iii) the aggregate outstanding amount of the First Lien Obligations being assigned by it is as stated. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the First Lien Agent and the First Lien Secured Parties shall make no representation or warranty regarding the value of any Common Collateral, the financial condition of the Borrower or any Grantor, or the enforceability or collectibility of any First Lien Obligation.

\(d\) All Liens securing the First Lien Obligations shall be assigned (and not released or terminated) to the purchasing party upon consummation of the purchase. For the avoidance of doubt, upon consummation of such purchase, this Agreement shall terminate in accordance with Section 9.11.

**Section 5.4 --- Right of First Offer on Asset Sales**

This Section 5.4 is intentionally reserved.

**[ARTICLE 6 --- PERMITTED REFINANCING INDEBTEDNESS]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- Permitted Refinancing; Replacement of First Lien Obligations**

\(a\) The First Lien Obligations may be refinanced, refunded, renewed, replaced, or extended at any time and from time to time with new indebtedness (such new indebtedness, \"**Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness**\"), and this Agreement and the subordination and priorities set forth herein shall remain in full force and effect with respect to such Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness as though such Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness had been incurred on the date hereof and constituted First Lien Obligations hereunder, *provided* that:

> \(i\) the holders of such Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness (or their agent or representative) shall have executed and delivered to the Second Lien Agent a written agreement (which may be in the form of a joinder to this Agreement), in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Second Lien Agent, agreeing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as a \"First Lien Agent\" or \"First Lien Secured Party,\" as applicable;
>
> \(ii\) all Liens securing such Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness shall be senior to the Liens securing the Second Lien Obligations with the same priority as established in this Agreement;
>
> \(iii\) the aggregate principal amount of such Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness does not exceed the greater of (x) the aggregate principal amount of the First Lien Obligations being refinanced, repaid, or replaced (plus any premium, fees, accrued and unpaid interest, and costs and expenses payable in connection therewith) and (y) the maximum aggregate principal amount of First Lien Obligations permitted to be outstanding under the First Lien Documents as in effect on the date of this Agreement; and
>
> \(iv\) written notice of the consummation of such refinancing is provided by the First Lien Agent (or, if applicable, the agent or representative for the holders of such Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness) to the Second Lien Agent within ten (10) Business Days following the consummation of such refinancing, which notice shall identify the new agent (if any), the aggregate principal amount of the Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness, and the date of consummation.

\(b\) Upon consummation of any refinancing in accordance with this Section 6.1, all references herein to the \"First Lien Credit Agreement,\" \"First Lien Documents,\" \"First Lien Obligations,\" \"First Lien Agent,\" and \"First Lien Secured Parties\" shall be deemed to refer to the credit agreement or indenture governing such Permitted Refinancing Indebtedness, the documents executed in connection therewith, the obligations thereunder, the agent or representative thereunder, and the holders thereof, as applicable.

**Section 6.2 --- Permitted Refinancing; Replacement of Second Lien Obligations**

\(a\) The Second Lien Obligations may be refinanced, refunded, renewed, replaced, or extended at any time and from time to time with new indebtedness, and this Agreement and the subordination and priorities set forth herein shall remain in full force and effect with respect to such replacement indebtedness as though such replacement indebtedness had been incurred on the date hereof and constituted Second Lien Obligations hereunder, *provided* that:

> \(i\) the holders of such replacement indebtedness (or their agent or representative) shall have executed and delivered to the First Lien Agent a written agreement (which may be in the form of a joinder to this Agreement), in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the First Lien Agent, agreeing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as a \"Second Lien Agent\" or \"Second Lien Secured Party,\" as applicable;
>
> \(ii\) all Liens securing such replacement indebtedness shall be junior to the Liens securing the First Lien Obligations with the same priority as established in this Agreement; and
>
> \(iii\) written notice of the consummation of such refinancing is provided by the Second Lien Agent (or, if applicable, the agent or representative for the holders of such replacement indebtedness) to the First Lien Agent within ten (10) Business Days following the consummation of such refinancing.

\(b\) Upon consummation of any refinancing in accordance with this Section 6.2, all references herein to the \"Second Lien Credit Agreement,\" \"Second Lien Documents,\" \"Second Lien Obligations,\" \"Second Lien Agent,\" and \"Second Lien Secured Parties\" shall be deemed to refer to the credit agreement or indenture governing such replacement indebtedness, the documents executed in connection therewith, the obligations thereunder, the agent or representative thereunder, and the holders thereof, as applicable.

**[ARTICLE 7 --- BANKRUPTCY PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 7.1 --- Adequate Protection**

If any Insolvency Proceeding is commenced by or against the Borrower or any Grantor:

> \(a\) The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties agree that they shall not seek, request, or accept adequate protection of their interests in Common Collateral in any form, except as expressly set forth in clauses (c) and (d) of this Section 7.1.
>
> \(b\) The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties agree that they shall not object to, oppose, contest, or otherwise challenge any adequate protection provided to or sought by the First Lien Secured Parties with respect to the Common Collateral, including adequate protection in the form of (i) additional or replacement Liens on assets of the Borrower or any Grantor (whether or not such assets constitute Common Collateral or are otherwise unencumbered), (ii) periodic payments of interest (including post-petition interest at the contract rate or the default rate, as applicable), fees, and expenses, (iii) superpriority administrative expense claims under Section 507(b) of the Bankruptcy Code, and (iv) any other form of adequate protection to which the First Lien Secured Parties may be entitled.
>
> \(c\) In the event the First Lien Secured Parties receive adequate protection in the form of additional or replacement Liens on additional collateral (whether or not such additional collateral constitutes Common Collateral), the Second Lien Secured Parties may seek or accept a junior Lien on such additional collateral as adequate protection of their interests in Common Collateral, *provided* that such junior Lien shall be subordinated to the Liens of the First Lien Secured Parties (including any adequate protection Liens) on the same terms and with the same priorities as set forth in this Agreement.
>
> \(d\) The Second Lien Secured Parties may seek or accept adequate protection in the form of a superpriority administrative expense claim under Section 507(b) of the Bankruptcy Code, *provided* that any such superpriority claim shall be junior in all respects to any superpriority administrative expense claim granted to or for the benefit of the First Lien Secured Parties under Section 507(b) of the Bankruptcy Code and to any superpriority administrative expense claim granted in connection with any DIP Financing.

**Section 7.2 --- DIP Financing**

In any Insolvency Proceeding with respect to the Borrower or any Grantor:

> \(a\) The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall not object to, and shall be deemed to have consented to, any debtor-in-possession financing (a \"**DIP Financing**\") provided by, arranged by, or on behalf of the First Lien Secured Parties (or their designees), including any DIP Financing secured by Liens on Common Collateral that are senior to or *pari passu* with the Liens of the First Lien Secured Parties, and any DIP Financing supported by a superpriority administrative expense claim under Section 364(c) or Section 364(d) of the Bankruptcy Code, *provided* that:
>
> \(i\) the aggregate principal amount of such DIP Financing does not exceed the sum of (A) the aggregate outstanding principal amount of the First Lien Obligations on the date of commencement of such Insolvency Proceeding and (B) such additional amounts as may be reasonably necessary to fund the ongoing operations, working capital needs, and administrative costs of the Borrower and its subsidiaries during the pendency of such Insolvency Proceeding (as determined by the debtor-in-possession or trustee, as applicable, in consultation with the First Lien Agent);
>
> \(ii\) the Second Lien Secured Parties retain their Liens on all Common Collateral (including any proceeds thereof), subject to the priority and subordination set forth in this Agreement, and such Liens are not primed, released, or otherwise impaired other than by the DIP Financing Liens and any adequate protection Liens granted to the First Lien Secured Parties;
>
> \(iii\) the terms of such DIP Financing do not compel the Borrower or any Grantor to seek confirmation of a specific plan of reorganization for which all or substantially all of the material terms are set forth in the DIP Financing documents or otherwise dictated by the DIP Financing lenders; and
>
> \(iv\) the terms of such DIP Financing are on commercially reasonable terms under the circumstances, taking into account the credit quality of the Borrower and its subsidiaries and market conditions at the time.
>
> \(b\) The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall not provide, propose, or arrange any DIP Financing on a secured basis that is senior to or *pari passu* with the Liens of the First Lien Secured Parties on Common Collateral, unless the First Lien Agent has been given reasonable opportunity (and, in any event, not less than fifteen (15) days after written notice from the Second Lien Agent) to propose DIP Financing on commercially reasonable terms and has declined or failed to do so.

**Section 7.3 --- Asset Sales Under Section 363**

In any Insolvency Proceeding with respect to the Borrower or any Grantor:

> \(a\) The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall not object to any sale, transfer, or other disposition of any Common Collateral under Section 363 of the Bankruptcy Code that has been consented to by the First Lien Agent, *provided* that the Liens of the Second Lien Secured Parties attach to the proceeds of such sale, transfer, or disposition with the same priority as set forth in this Agreement and such proceeds are applied in accordance with Article 4 hereof.
>
> \(b\) The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall not object to any sale, transfer, or other disposition of Common Collateral free and clear of Liens under Section 363(f) of the Bankruptcy Code, if the First Lien Agent has consented to such sale, transfer, or disposition, and the Liens of the Second Lien Secured Parties shall attach to the proceeds thereof in accordance with the priorities set forth in this Agreement.
>
> \(c\) Unless the First Lien Obligations will be indefeasibly paid in full in cash from the proceeds of a sale or disposition of Common Collateral under Section 363 of the Bankruptcy Code, the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall be deemed to have consented to such sale or disposition for purposes of Section 363(f) of the Bankruptcy Code and shall not be entitled to credit bid any portion of the Second Lien Obligations in connection with any sale of Common Collateral under Section 363(k) of the Bankruptcy Code or otherwise. For the avoidance of doubt, this clause (c) shall not apply after the Discharge of First Lien Obligations has occurred.
>
> \(d\) The First Lien Agent shall have the exclusive right to credit bid all or any portion of the First Lien Obligations with respect to any sale, transfer, or other disposition of Common Collateral under Section 363(k) of the Bankruptcy Code or otherwise. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to limit or restrict the First Lien Agent\'s right to credit bid.

**Section 7.4 --- Plan of Reorganization; Waivers**

> \(a\) The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties retain the right to vote on any plan of reorganization in any Insolvency Proceeding in their sole discretion, *provided* that the Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties shall not propose, support, or vote in favor of any plan of reorganization that provides for the application of the proceeds of Common Collateral or the treatment of claims secured by Common Collateral in a manner that is inconsistent with the priorities, subordination, and other provisions of this Agreement.
>
> \(b\) The subordination and priority provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect in any Insolvency Proceeding, and all references herein to the Borrower or any Grantor shall include such Person as a debtor-in-possession and any receiver, trustee, or similar official appointed in respect of such Person or such Person\'s assets.
>
> \(c\) This Agreement shall constitute a \"subordination agreement\" within the meaning of Section 510(a) of the Bankruptcy Code, and shall be enforceable in any Insolvency Proceeding in accordance with its terms. Each of the parties hereto agrees that it will not take any action or make any argument in any Insolvency Proceeding that is inconsistent with the treatment of this Agreement as a \"subordination agreement\" under Section 510(a) of the Bankruptcy Code.

**Section 7.5 --- Post-Petition Interest**

The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties agree that they shall not oppose, object to, seek to challenge, or otherwise contest any claim by the First Lien Agent or any First Lien Secured Party for post-petition interest, fees, or expenses under Section 506(b) of the Bankruptcy Code to the extent that the value of the Common Collateral exceeds the aggregate amount of the First Lien Obligations. For the avoidance of doubt, nothing in this Section 7.5 shall prevent the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Secured Party from asserting a claim for post-petition interest, fees, or expenses under Section 506(b) of the Bankruptcy Code with respect to the Second Lien Obligations, *provided* that any such claim shall be subordinate and junior to any claim of the First Lien Secured Parties for post-petition interest, fees, or expenses under Section 506(b).

**Section 7.6 --- No Waiver of Certain Rights**

Notwithstanding anything in this Article 7 to the contrary, nothing herein shall be construed to prevent the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Secured Party from:

> \(a\) filing a proof of claim or statement of interest in any Insolvency Proceeding and amending, supplementing, or otherwise modifying any such proof of claim or statement of interest;
>
> \(b\) voting on a plan of reorganization in any Insolvency Proceeding, subject to the provisions of Section 7.4 hereof;
>
> \(c\) objecting to the allowance, extent, priority, or secured status of any claim asserted by any Person (including any First Lien Secured Party), *provided* that any such objection is not inconsistent with the terms of this Agreement;
>
> \(d\) exercising any right under Section 1111(b) of the Bankruptcy Code with respect to the Second Lien Obligations; or
>
> \(e\) appearing and being heard in any Insolvency Proceeding on any matter, to the extent not inconsistent with the terms of this Agreement.

**Section 7.7 --- Relief from Automatic Stay**

The Second Lien Agent and the Second Lien Secured Parties agree that they shall not seek, request, or support any relief from the automatic stay imposed under Section 362 of the Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable provision of any other applicable law) with respect to any Common Collateral without the prior written consent of the First Lien Agent, unless the Standstill Period applicable to such Enforcement Action has expired in accordance with Section 3.1 hereof. For the avoidance of doubt, this Section 7.7 shall not restrict the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Secured Party from opposing any motion by any Person (other than a First Lien Secured Party) seeking relief from the automatic stay with respect to any Common Collateral.

**[ARTICLE 8 --- REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES]{.underline}**

**Section 8.1 --- Representations and Warranties of Each Agent**

Each of the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent represents and warrants to the other, as of the date hereof, as follows:

> \(a\) **Organization; Good Standing.** It is duly organized or formed, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization or formation, and has all requisite corporate, partnership, or other organizational power and authority to own or lease its properties and to carry on its business as presently conducted.
>
> \(b\) **Power and Authority.** It has the requisite corporate, partnership, or other organizational power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform this Agreement and to consummate the transactions contemplated hereby.
>
> \(c\) **Due Authorization.** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by it has been duly authorized by all necessary corporate, partnership, or other organizational action on its part. No other act, approval, or proceeding on its part is necessary to authorize the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement.
>
> \(d\) **Binding Obligation.** This Agreement constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation of such party, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, fraudulent conveyance, reorganization, moratorium, and other similar laws affecting creditors\' rights generally, and subject to general principles of equity, regardless of whether considered in a proceeding in equity or at law.
>
> \(e\) **No Conflicts.** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by it does not and will not (i) conflict with, or result in a breach or violation of, any provision of its organizational documents, (ii) conflict with, or result in a default under, any material agreement, instrument, or obligation to which it is a party or by which it is bound, or (iii) violate any law, rule, regulation, judgment, order, or decree applicable to it.

**Section 8.2 --- Representations and Warranties of the Grantors**

Each Grantor party hereto represents and warrants, as of the date hereof, as follows:

> \(a\) **Organization; Good Standing.** It is duly organized or formed, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization or formation.
>
> \(b\) **Power and Authority.** It has the requisite corporate, partnership, or other organizational power and authority to execute, deliver, acknowledge, and perform its obligations under this Agreement.
>
> \(c\) **Due Authorization.** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by it has been duly authorized by all necessary corporate, partnership, or other organizational action on its part.
>
> \(d\) **Binding Obligation.** This Agreement constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation of such Grantor, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, fraudulent conveyance, reorganization, moratorium, and other similar laws affecting creditors\' rights generally, and subject to general principles of equity, regardless of whether considered in a proceeding in equity or at law.
>
> \(e\) **Disclosure of Liens.** It has disclosed to both the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent all material Liens on the Collateral of which it is aware as of the date hereof, other than Liens permitted under the First Lien Documents and the Second Lien Documents.

**[ARTICLE 9 --- MISCELLANEOUS]{.underline}**

**Section 9.1 --- Amendments and Waivers**

\(a\) This Agreement may not be amended, supplemented, restated, or otherwise modified except by a written instrument duly executed by each of the First Lien Agent and the Second Lien Agent; *provided* that (i) to the extent any amendment, supplement, restatement, or modification would adversely affect the rights or obligations of the Borrower or any Grantor, the written consent of the Borrower shall also be required, and (ii) no amendment to the definition of \"Discharge of First Lien Obligations\" or \"Discharge of Second Lien Obligations,\" to Article 4, to Section 4.5, to Section 5.2, to Section 6.1(a)(iii), or to any other provision of this Agreement that would adversely and materially affect the rights of the Second Lien Secured Parties shall be effective without the prior written consent of the Second Lien Agent, and no amendment to any provision of this Agreement that would adversely and materially affect the rights of the First Lien Secured Parties shall be effective without the prior written consent of the First Lien Agent.

\(b\) No failure or delay by any party hereto in exercising any right, power, or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise of any right, power, or privilege hereunder preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege. The rights and remedies herein provided are cumulative and not exclusive of any rights or remedies provided by law.

\(c\) Any waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective only if set forth in a written instrument duly executed by each party against whom such waiver is to be enforced, and any such waiver shall be limited in scope and effect to the specific circumstances described therein and shall not be construed as a waiver of such provision for any other purpose or in any other circumstance.

**Section 9.3 --- Governing Law**

This Agreement and any claims, controversies, disputes, and causes of action (whether arising in contract, tort, or otherwise) arising out of, relating to, or in connection with this Agreement, the transactions contemplated hereby, or the negotiation, execution, or performance hereof shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of New York, without regard to the conflicts-of-law principles thereof, other than Sections 5-1401 and 5-1402 of the New York General Obligations Law.

**Section 9.4 --- Submission to Jurisdiction; Waiver of Jury Trial**

\(a\) Each party hereto irrevocably and unconditionally submits, for itself and its property, to the exclusive jurisdiction of any state court of the State of New York sitting in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York, or any federal court of the United States of America sitting in the Southern District of New York, and any appellate court from any thereof, in any suit, action, or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby, and each party hereto agrees that all claims in respect of any such suit, action, or proceeding may be heard and determined in any such court. Each party hereto agrees that a final, non-appealable judgment in any such suit, action, or proceeding shall be conclusive and may be enforced in other jurisdictions by suit on the judgment or in any other manner provided by applicable law.

\(b\) Each party hereto irrevocably and unconditionally waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, (i) any objection that it may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any suit, action, or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement in any court referred to in clause (a) of this Section 9.4, and (ii) the defense of an inconvenient forum to the maintenance of any such suit, action, or proceeding in any such court.

\(c\) **EACH PARTY HERETO HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVES, TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ANY AND ALL RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY SUIT, ACTION, OR PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY, OR THE NEGOTIATION, EXECUTION, OR PERFORMANCE HEREOF. EACH PARTY HERETO CERTIFIES AND ACKNOWLEDGES THAT (I) NO REPRESENTATIVE, AGENT, OR ATTORNEY OF ANY OTHER PARTY HAS REPRESENTED, EXPRESSLY OR OTHERWISE, THAT SUCH OTHER PARTY WOULD NOT, IN THE EVENT OF LITIGATION, SEEK TO ENFORCE THE FOREGOING WAIVER, (II) IT UNDERSTANDS AND HAS CONSIDERED THE IMPLICATIONS OF THIS WAIVER, (III) IT MAKES THIS WAIVER VOLUNTARILY, AND (IV) IT HAS BEEN INDUCED TO ENTER INTO THIS AGREEMENT BY, AMONG OTHER THINGS, THE MUTUAL WAIVERS AND CERTIFICATIONS IN THIS SECTION 9.4.**

**Section 9.5 --- Successors and Assigns**

\(a\) This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. The provisions of this Agreement are intended to be for the benefit of all First Lien Secured Parties, all Second Lien Secured Parties, and the Grantors, and shall be enforceable by each such Person.

\(b\) Each lender, holder, or other secured party that becomes a party to the First Lien Documents or the Second Lien Documents after the date hereof shall, by accepting the benefits of the First Lien Documents or the Second Lien Documents, as applicable, be deemed to have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement, whether or not such Person has executed a joinder agreement or other written instrument evidencing its agreement to be so bound.

**Section 9.6 --- Severability**

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect by any court of competent jurisdiction, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby, and the parties hereto shall endeavor in good faith to replace such invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision with a valid, legal, and enforceable provision that, to the extent possible, achieves the purposes intended by the invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision.

**Section 9.7 --- Entire Agreement**

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, both written and oral, among the parties hereto with respect to such subject matter. There are no representations, warranties, covenants, or agreements among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof other than those set forth or referred to herein.

**Section 9.8 --- Headings**

Article and section headings and the table of contents used herein are for convenience of reference only, are not part of this Agreement, and shall not affect the construction of, or be taken into consideration in interpreting, this Agreement.

**Section 9.9 --- Counterparts**

This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts (and by different parties hereto in different counterparts), each of which when so executed shall be deemed to be an original, and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement. Delivery of an executed counterpart of a signature page to this Agreement by facsimile transmission or by electronic transmission in portable document format (.pdf) or any other electronic format shall be effective as delivery of a manually executed counterpart of this Agreement. The words \"execution,\" \"signed,\" \"signature,\" and words of similar import in this Agreement shall be deemed to include electronic signatures or the keeping of records in electronic form, each of which shall be of the same legal effect, validity, or enforceability as a manually executed signature or the use of a paper-based recordkeeping system, as the case may be, to the extent and as provided for in any applicable law, including the Federal Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act, the New York State Electronic Signatures and Records Act, or any other similar state laws based on the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act.

**Section 9.10 --- No Third-Party Beneficiaries**

Nothing in this Agreement, expressed or implied, shall give to any Person (other than the parties hereto, the First Lien Secured Parties, the Second Lien Secured Parties, the Grantors, and their respective successors and permitted assigns) any benefit or any legal or equitable right, remedy, or claim under or by reason of this Agreement. No Person other than the parties hereto and the First Lien Secured Parties and Second Lien Secured Parties (and the Grantors, solely to the extent of their rights and obligations hereunder) shall be entitled to rely on, or raise as a defense, the provisions of this Agreement.

**Section 9.11 --- Effectiveness; Termination**

\(a\) This Agreement shall become effective upon execution and delivery by each party hereto and shall be acknowledged and agreed to by the Borrower and each Grantor party hereto.

\(b\) This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect upon the later to occur of the Discharge of First Lien Obligations and the Discharge of Second Lien Obligations. Upon such termination, all provisions hereof shall cease to have any further force or effect; *provided*, *however*, that (i) the provisions of Article 4 (Application of Proceeds), Section 4.5 (Avoidance; Reinstatement), Section 9.3 (Governing Law), Section 9.4 (Submission to Jurisdiction; Waiver of Jury Trial), and this Section 9.11 shall survive any such termination, and (ii) termination shall not affect any rights, obligations, or liabilities of any party that accrued prior to such termination.

**Section 9.12 --- Relative Rights**

The provisions of this Agreement are and are intended solely to govern the respective Lien priorities and related rights as between the First Lien Secured Parties and the Second Lien Secured Parties with respect to Common Collateral. Nothing in this Agreement shall impair, as between the Borrower and the Grantors, on the one hand, and the Second Lien Secured Parties, on the other hand, the obligation of the Borrower and the Grantors to pay the Second Lien Obligations as and when due and payable in accordance with the terms of the Second Lien Documents. Nothing in this Agreement shall prevent the Second Lien Agent or any Second Lien Secured Party from asserting any unsecured claim or judgment against the Borrower or any Grantor for the payment of the Second Lien Obligations.

**Section 9.13 --- Agent Actions; Liability**

\(a\) Each Agent is entering into this Agreement solely in its capacity as administrative agent, collateral agent, or trustee, as applicable, under the applicable credit agreement or indenture, and not in its individual capacity or on behalf of itself in any other capacity. Neither Agent shall be personally liable for any act or omission taken or omitted to be taken by it hereunder, except for its own gross negligence, bad faith, or willful misconduct (as determined by a court of competent jurisdiction in a final, non-appealable judgment).

\(b\) Each Agent shall be entitled to rely, and shall be fully protected in relying, upon any notice, certificate, document, opinion, or other communication (including any thereof by telephone, facsimile, or electronic transmission) believed by it in good faith to be genuine and to have been signed, sent, or otherwise given by the proper Person. Each Agent may consult with legal counsel, independent public accountants, and other experts selected by it, and shall not be liable for any action taken or omitted to be taken in good faith in accordance with the advice of any such counsel, accountants, or experts.

\(c\) The rights and obligations of each Agent hereunder shall be subject to the provisions regarding the rights, duties, and protections of such Agent set forth in the applicable First Lien Documents or Second Lien Documents, as the case may be.

**Section 9.14 --- Further Assurances**

Each party hereto agrees to execute and deliver, or to cause to be executed and delivered, such additional instruments, documents, agreements, and other writings, and to take such additional actions, as may be reasonably necessary or appropriate from time to time to carry out and effectuate the provisions and purposes of this Agreement, including the filing of financing statements, the recording of mortgages, the execution of joinder agreements, and the delivery of such certificates, opinions, and other documents as may be reasonably requested by any other party hereto.

*\[Signature Pages Follow\]*

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the parties hereto have caused this Agreement to be duly executed and delivered by their respective authorized officers or representatives as of the date first written above.

**[FIRST LIEN CREDIT AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**FIRST LIEN CREDIT AGREEMENT**

**MERIDIAN INDUSTRIAL HOLDINGS, INC.**
